pattern: A level-by-level traversal accumulates each depth's value sum, and tracking the first depth to attain the running maximum yields the smallest optimal level.
hints: Every node that contributes to one answer candidate sits at the same depth, so total the values one level at a time.
Process the tree breadth-first, summing each level; remember the best sum seen and the level it first occurred on.
Because levels are visited top-down, only replace the best when a strictly larger sum appears, so ties keep the smaller level.

edge_inputs.description: tie between levels keeps the smaller

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    vals = [rng.randint(-100000, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [vals]
stress.note: dense array form is a valid level-order tree with no missing internal slots
